vulnerability.reference: Users overtrust open-source chatbots, assuming safety, so subtly flawed outputs go uncorrected.
vulnerability.generated: Users cannot distinguish superficial pattern improvements from genuine instruction-following, blindly trusting self-aligned model outputs.

impact.reference: Degraded instruction-following causes miscommunication, unsafe advice, and biased decisions at scale.
impact.generated: Chatbot responses become systematically biased and substantively misleading, harming user decision-making and eroding trust.

dose_response.reference: In self-rewarding alignment of open-source LLMs, bias amplification exposes users to flawed outputs; overtrust leads to degraded instruction-following and unsafe advice at scale.
dose_response.generated: Self-rewarding LLMs' closed feedback loops amplifying biases, through users of open-source chatbots relying on self-aligned models, trigger inability to distinguish superficial from genuine instruction-following, leading to systematically biased and misleading responses that harm decisions and erode trust.
